language_elements.value:
An agile leg mimicing Earth insects, allowing robots to stably walk on walls and beneath overhangs.

Press XXX to jump. Hold XXX to crouch, charging a boosted jump. Continue holding XXX after jumping to skate after landing.

language_elements.name: block-mod_dash-desc
language_elements.value:
On activation, create a singularity that displaces bot in desired direction.

Stacking improves energy consumption.

language_elements.name: block-mod_flare-desc
language_elements.value:
On activation, grants temporary immunity to lock-ons and deploys a field that destroys nova projectiles and missiles.

Stacking grants additional charges.

language_elements.name: block-mod_fuel-desc
language_elements.value:
On activation, expend turbofuel to tremendously increase thrust output of thrusters that align with the module.

Stacking improves energy consumption.

language_elements.name: block-mod_shockwave-desc
language_elements.value:
On activation, release a radial blast that damages and pushes enemy bots away.

Stacking increases blast force and radius.

language_elements.name: block-mod_overshield-desc
language_elements.value:
Passively creates a full-bot overshield that takes incoming damage before the bot does, with a slow recharge.

Stacking grants additional overshield.

language_elements.name: block-mod_spoofer-desc
language_elements.value:
On activation, temporarily disguise your IFF signal to appear as an ally to your enemies, fooling mines and missile locks as well. Taking and dealing damage drains the effect faster.

Stacking grants additional disguide time.

language_elements.name: block-mod_forge-desc
language_elements.value:
Passively restocks secondary weapon systems.

Stacking grants additional restock speed.

language_elements.name: block-ammo_box_small-desc
language_elements.value: Provides a small increase of reserve ammunition to supplement your equipped secondary weapon system.

language_elements.name: block-ammo_box_large-desc
language_elements.value: Provides a large increase of reserve ammunition to supplement your equipped secondary weapon system.

language_elements.name: block-energy_capacitor_small-desc
language_elements.value: Provides a small increase of available energy to power your equipped modules.

language_elements.name: block-energy_capacitor_large-desc
language_elements.value: Provides a large increase of available energy to power your equipped modules.

language_elements.name: tech-1-desc
language_elements.value:
A reliable mid-range cannon good for all engagements. All primaries have infinite ammunition. 

Osprey standard issue.

language_elements.name: tech-2-desc
language_elements.value: A long-distance, high velocity sniper cannon with a shell-loading magazine.

language_elements.name: tech-3-desc
language_elements.value: A recharging artillery cannon that fires a volley of unstable energy payloads.

language_elements.name: tech-4-desc
language_elements.value: A continuous beam weapon that repairs allied bots and damages enemies.

language_elements.name: tech-5-desc
language_elements.value: A mid-range improvised cannon that fires superheated rivets from a magazine

language_elements.name: tech-6-desc
language_elements.value: A mid-range cannon that fires a manually-loaded volley of unguided rockets.

language_elements.name: tech-51-desc
language_elements.value:
Drops an anti-tank proximity mine on use. All secondaries reload automatically from their ammunition store. 

Osprey standard issue.

language_elements.name: tech-52-desc
language_elements.value: Ammo crates that expand your secondary weapon ammo stores.

language_elements.name: tech-53-desc
language_elements.value: A small cannon that launches disruptive smoke. Blocks enemy vision and spotting.

language_elements.name: tech-54-desc
language_elements.value: A fixed weapon that launches a fire-and-forget heat-seeking missile. Vulnerable to flares.

language_elements.name: tech-55-desc
language_elements.value: A fixed weapon that fires armor-piercing rounds at a high rate of fire.

language_elements.name: tech-56-desc
language_elements.value: A fixed melee weapon that deals contact damage to enemy bots. Unstable.

language_elements.name: tech-101-desc
language_elements.value:
Simple wheels for navigating ground terrain. Comes in steering and non-steering variants. 

Osprey standard issue.

language_elements.name: tech-102-desc
language_elements.value: Durable and reliable ground mobility with two different operating gears.

language_elements.name: tech-103-desc
language_elements.value: Low-hovering ground mobility with strafe capabilities.

language_elements.name: tech-104-desc
language_elements.value: An advanced mechanical leg capable of anchoring to walls and ceilings as well as perform actions such as strafing, crouching, and leaping.

language_elements.name: tech-105-desc
language_elements.value: A larger mechanical leg with heavier armor and regenerating shields, but lower mobility.

language_elements.name: tech-106-desc
language_elements.value: A small and nimble mechanical leg with lesser load capacity, but built with redundancy in mind.

language_elements.name: tech-107-desc
language_elements.value: Simple skis that provide no propulsion, but have limited friction.

language_elements.name: tech-108-desc
language_elements.value: Advanced repulsion pads that provide no propulsion, but have very little friction.

language_elements.name: tech-109-desc
language_elements.value:
A small impulse thruster for providing propulsion and control on a variety of craft. 

Osprey standard issue.

language_elements.name: tech-110-desc
language_elements.value: A more compact but power-hungry version of the medium thruster, exchanging AMP efficiency for internal space savings.

language_elements.name: tech-111-desc
language_elements.value: A large impulse thruster for providing propulsion and control on a variety of craft.

language_elements.name: tech-112-desc
language_elements.value: A powerful, throttle-controlled thruster for providing main propulsion for aircraft. Can deflect thrust for manual and automatic thrust vectoring.

language_elements.name: tech-113-desc
language_elements.value: A medium impulse thruster for providing propulsion and control on a variety of craft.

language_elements.name: tech-114-desc
language_elements.value: Small Lighter Than Air (LTA) canisters that provide ballast-controlled buoyancy, drag, and lift.

language_elements.name: tech-115-desc
language_elements.value: Larger LTA canisters with greater lift capacity. Capable of creating blimps.

language_elements.name: tech-116-desc
language_elements.value:
Stable, high lift wings with moderate drag. Combine with thrusters and a balanced Center of Mass to achieve flight.

Comes with the Swept Rudder, which provide additional drag and control.

language_elements.name: tech-117-desc
language_elements.value:
Simple wings with good low-speed control and modest lift. Prone to diminished control from overspeed.

Comes with the Flat Rudder.

language_elements.name: tech-118-desc
language_elements.value:
Unstable wings with great high speed performance and control, but prone to stalls. Lift performance is reliant on high speeds.

Comes with the Delta Rudder.

language_elements.name: tech-201-desc
language_elements.value:
Basic building blocks of standard, heavy, and light weights. Used to create bot hulls. 

Osprey standard issue.

language_elements.name: tech-202-desc
language_elements.value: Cosmetic variants of the light and standard blocks, for a company-approved level of personal expression.

language_elements.name: tech-203-desc
language_elements.value: A collection of lightweight rods featuring connections on both ends.

language_elements.name: tech-204-desc
language_elements.value: A collection of angled and oddly shaped rods featuring connections on both ends

language_elements.value:
A deprecated collection of regenerating energy shields to protect specific points on your bot.

You probably shouldn't be seeing this anymore.

language_elements.value: A collection of regenerating energy shields to protect specific points on your bot.

language_elements.name: tech-207-desc
language_elements.value: An expanded collection of regenerating energy shields to protect specific points on your bot.

language_elements.value: On activation, helps counteract bot rotation.

language_elements.value: On activation, grants temporary immunity to lock-ons and deploys a field that destroys nova projectiles and missiles.

language_elements.value: On activation, expend turbofuel to tremendously increase thrust output of thrusters that align with the module.

language_elements.name: tech-254-desc
language_elements.value: On activation, release a radial blast that damages and pushes enemy bots away.

language_elements.name: tech-255-desc
language_elements.value: Passively creates a full-bot overshield that takes incoming damage before the bot does, with a slow recharge.

language_elements.name: tech-256-desc

language_elements.name: tech-257-desc

language_elements.name: tut-paused-message
language_elements.value: Did you want to quit the Procelio User Orientation? ⚠ O.P.A.L. will remember this.

language_elements.name: tut-intro-1
language_elements.value: Welcome to the Procelio User Orientation! We’re happy to have you here.

language_elements.name: tut-intro-2
language_elements.value: I am the Osprey Productivity Alignment Logician, OPAL for short. I will be your company-mandated guide through the Procelio Remote Robotics Program.

language_elements.name: tut-intro-3
language_elements.value: Please press the highlighted 'Edit Robot' button (or ${ShortcutEscape} on your input device) to enter editing mode.

language_elements.name: tut-garage-intro
language_elements.value: This is the garage environment where you can build, modify, and test robots. Lets get you acclimated with your Osprey Augmented Testing tools.

language_elements.name: tut-garage-cam
language_elements.value: I’ve provided a helpful checklist to calibrate your camera movement. Try each option out and get a feel for how to navigate the garage.

language_elements.name: tut-garage-bots
language_elements.value: Good work! Your robots- ‘bots,’ affectionately- are remotely piloted vehicles which you'll use to participate in assigned missions.

language_elements.name: tut-garage-bots-2
language_elements.value:
‘Assigned missions’ involve approved levels of robot combat, which means your bots should be armed. This one here, however, doesn't have any guns yet.

Let's remedy that!

language_elements.name: tut-reqtree-1
language_elements.value: Press ${ShortcutBuild2Tech} to open the Requisitions Tree.

language_elements.name: tut-reqtree-2
language_elements.value: This is the Requisitions Tree, where you can use company credits to unlock licenses to procure and operate new equipment.

language_elements.name: tut-reqtree-3
language_elements.value:
To the left of the tree are the category tabs. Navigate to the Weaponry tab and unlock the Electrolaser Carbine.

Click on a node to see its details. Unlock it using the right details pane.

language_elements.name: tut-reqtree-4
language_elements.value: Great! Now that you’re licensed to kill, press ${ShortcutBuild2Tech} or ${ShortcutEscape} to return to build mode.

language_elements.name: tut-garage-guns
language_elements.value: Now let's get this bot armed. Use ${GaragePlaceBlock} to place the provided Electrolaser Carbines on top of highlighted blocks.

language_elements.name: tut-garage-movement-1
language_elements.value:
Good work! Your bot has guns, but is still lacking movement.

You won’t be able to operate at minimum acceptable standards with it like this.

language_elements.name: tut-garage-movement-2
language_elements.value: Fortunately, as a Zulu-grade employee in the Osprey Contracting Division, you've been pre-approved for access to modular wheels.

language_elements.name: tut-inventory-2
language_elements.value:
This is the Inventory, where you can view and select parts you’ve unlocked the license to use.

Navigate to the Movement tab at the top bar and select Steering Wheels

language_elements.name: tut-garage-movement-3
language_elements.value:
Now that you have selected them, place wheels on the green blocks on the bots undercarriage. 

Tip: you can press ${GarageToggleMirrorActive} to toggle automatic mirrored build actions off and on.

language_elements.name: tut-core
language_elements.value: Great work! In addition to guns and movements, all bots need a core to provide critical function. This bot already has a core installed, but the core is the heart of your robot- don't leave it out in the open!

language_elements.name: tut-deleting-blocks
language_elements.value:
Now you’re all ready to start driving- 

Oops, sorry, I spilled some blocks.

language_elements.name: tut-deleting-blocks-2
language_elements.value: To be able to use a bot, all blocks must be connected to the core of the robot. Any disconnected blocks will be automatically highlighted in red. Use ${GarageRemoveBlock} to remove the excess blocks at the front of the garage.

language_elements.name: tut-deleting-blocks-3
language_elements.value: Great work! Osprey reminds you that a key factor in employee success is the willingness to clean up someone else's mess without asking questions.

language_elements.name: tut-drive-intro
language_elements.value:
With that out of the way, are you ready to drive?

Press ${ShortcutBuild2Test} to enter test mode.

language_elements.name: tut-drive-controls
language_elements.value:
I’ve provided a helpful checklist to calibrate your robot movement. Try each option out and get a feel for how to drive.

Tip: Wheeled bots have a turning radius. Try combining turning with forward and/or backward inputs.

language_elements.name: tut-drive-waypoint
language_elements.value: That’s it! Now drive to and park in the highlighted waypoint.

language_elements.name: tut-drive-home
language_elements.value:
This is the Osprey HOME environment: one of the company's testing locations for modular robots prior to deployment. 

You may want to take a moment to look around, this is where you will be testing all your bots going forward.

language_elements.name: tut-drive-compass
language_elements.value: Lets talk about navigation. At the bottom of your display is a company-standard navigation console displaying a compass, as well your bots' current speed, altitude, and orientation.

language_elements.name: tut-minimap-1
language_elements.value:
The Minimap in the top-left of your screen presents an overhead view of the environment, your position and heading within it, allied positions, and any nearby enemies detected on radar.

Press ${UIMinimapToggle} to hide the minimap.

language_elements.name: tut-minimap-2
language_elements.value:
Great!

Now press ${UIMinimapToggle} again to bring the minimap up again.

language_elements.name: tut-minimap-3
language_elements.value:
The minimap permits three levels of magnification.

Press ${UIMinimapScale} to cycle through them.

language_elements.name: tut-spot
language_elements.value:
In missions, sharing information is key. I've gone ahead and spawned a passive enemy bot ahead of you. Aim at it and use ${BattleEntitySpot} to 'spot' it.

Spotting an enemy will relay its position and information to your team, but there's a cooldown if you miss.

language_elements.name: tut-shooting
language_elements.value:
Great, weapons armed! Go shoot at that spotted bot until it explodes.

Press ${DriveShoot} to shoot, and hold ${DriveScope} to scope in for more precise aim.

Tip: the Electrolaser Carbine has infinite ammunition, but its accuracy decreases the longer ${DriveShoot} is held!

language_elements.name: tut-damage-intro
language_elements.value: Great work! You might have noticed parts falling off left and right as you shot that dummy. Let’s talk about damaging Osprey products.

language_elements.name: tut-damage-1
language_elements.value:
Damage in Procelio travels between parts along connection points.

If a part receives more damage than it has health, the remaining damage will transfer to adjacent connected parts as the part is destroyed.

language_elements.name: tut-damage-2
language_elements.value: I've projected a cross section of a bot to help demonstrate this damage flow system.

language_elements.name: tut-damage-3
language_elements.value: Shoot the highlighted red block and observe its effects.

language_elements.name: tut-damage-4
language_elements.value: Did you see the blocks connected to the red block also fall off? That was due to damage overflow!

language_elements.name: tut-damage-5
language_elements.value: Different weapons do different amounts of damage, but all damage follows the same basic rules. Clever building can help reduce the collateral damage of one well-placed shot.

language_elements.name: tut-damage-6
language_elements.value: As a modular structure, your robot will fall apart in different ways depending on how you build it and where you’re shot at. It’s not possible or expected to plan for every outcome, but understanding how damage works can help keep you in a fight longer.

language_elements.name: tut-damage-7
language_elements.value: This is important: Remember when I said the core is the heart of your robot? All bots must have exactly one core, and all components must be connected to it to be eligible for deployment.

language_elements.name: tut-damage-8
language_elements.value: As such, any parts that become disconnected from the core due to structural damage will be automatically destroyed.

language_elements.name: tut-damage-9
language_elements.value: Let's demonstrate this: please shoot the highlighted orange block and observe its effects.

language_elements.name: tut-damage-10
language_elements.value:
Look at all that damage!

Because a load-bearing part was shot, all of the parts that were connected to the core through it were also destroyed.

language_elements.name: tut-damage-11
language_elements.value: As exterior blocks of your bot are destroyed, the internals will become visible. Redundant connection routes to important parts can reduce the chances of suffering critical hits like that.

language_elements.name: tut-damage-12
language_elements.value: If all functional blocks- generally, guns or movement parts- are destroyed on a bot, its core will overload and the bot will explode. When in combat, shoot for the vital components to disable an opponent!

language_elements.name: tut-damage-13
language_elements.value: Now, shoot the yellow block and observe its effects.

language_elements.name: tut-damage-14
language_elements.value:
KaBOOM!

Great work, we’re almost done with your orientation.

language_elements.name: tut-abilities
language_elements.value: I've given you an activatable ability. Certain modules or game states may grant you these. Press ${DriveModule1} to trigger this one.

language_elements.name: tut-flip
language_elements.value:
Was that a ruse to get you flipped over? Why yes, yes it was.

You can use ${DriveFlipBot} to activate your built-in 'flip' ability to turn yourself right side up. Holding ${DriveFlipBot} will allow you to laterally rotate your bot before it's dropped back down.

language_elements.name: tut-weapon-swap
language_elements.value:
One more topic: weapons.
Each bot is permitted to have one category of Primary Weapon (like the Electrolaser Carbine) and one category of Secondary Weapon, in addition to up to four module types.

Your bot is equipped with minelayers as a secondary weapon. Press ${DriveCycleWeapon} to cycle the active weapon, then ${DriveShoot} to lay a mine.

language_elements.name: tut-aircraft-intro
language_elements.value: Great work! With that, there should be nothing else to challenge your rising career-

language_elements.name: tut-aircraft-1
language_elements.value: aaaAAA I forgot about air bots. Look up! Look UP!

language_elements.name: tut-aircraft-2
language_elements.value:
That's not good. It’s laughing at your damaged state from on high.

Go into the healing zone on the far side of this room to get repaired, then we’ll deal with it.

Tip: In online games, heal points can be found at your team's base.

language_elements.name: tut-aircraft-3
language_elements.value: Great! Now that you’re repaired, go and blast that drone out of the sky!

language_elements.name: tut-aircraft-outro
language_elements.value:
Good work. Always remember to be on the lookout, not everyone is fighting on the same playing field.

Aircraft can be very dangerous, but they also tend to have to retreat when sustaining damage.

language_elements.name: tut-outro-1
language_elements.value: Congratulations! You have completed the Procelio User Orientation and have been certified to possess the base-level competency expected of Osprey employees!

language_elements.name: tut-outro-2
language_elements.value:
There’s a lot to take in when it comes to modular robotic combat, we hope this orientation has provided you with the basic fundamentals for success.

OPAL will be deprecated if it has not!

language_elements.name: tut-outro-3
language_elements.value: Should you ever need to find more information about your environment, parts, and job duties, please refer to your Osprey Employee Logbook available in the garage menu.

language_elements.name: tut-outro-4
language_elements.value: We hope you’ve enjoyed your stay, you will be returned to the employee lounge shortly.

language_elements.name: prefab-desc-00
language_elements.value: A small trainer bot designed for introducing new pilots of the Procelio program to their controls. The Rookie is a minimum viable product for Osprey contractors and is considered ineffective for frontline combat, but is quite affordable and comes in over 16 million colors.

language_elements.name: prefab-desc-01
language_elements.value: An attempt to match evolving battlefield conditions, the Firestarter is a minimum viable product with a focus on surviving heavy ordnance impacts to its exterior ablative shell.

language_elements.name: prefab-desc-02
language_elements.value:
A medium-weight hovercraft designed for performing routine patrols and light combat duty. Strafe around corners and keep the nose on target to shred enemies with autocannon fire.

Designed by BreakfastBaron.

language_elements.name: prefab-desc-03
language_elements.value:
A mobile combat engineering and repair platform deployed by Osprey to support other frontline units. With a Flux Weaver array, a mine factory, and all-around shielding, the Tortoise is a highly valuable team player.

Designed by DestroyThing.

language_elements.name: prefab-desc-04
language_elements.value:
A simple yet reliable bot, the Marbler is a heavy-duty unit designed to outlast opponents over contested areas with heavy ballistic projection and reliable tank track movement. Its complexity is intentionally minimized to make it easy to manufacture and maintain.

Designed by siiftun1857.

language_elements.name: prefab-desc-05
language_elements.value:
An unconventionally asymmetrical design drafted in a late night fever, the Quasimodo leverages the unique strafing properties of wheeled craft to deliver and maintain a broadside of pain to other grounded bots. Recommended for more experienced pilots.

Designed by BoneBreaker.

language_elements.name: prefab-desc-06
language_elements.value:
A heavy bomber designed for high-altitude bombardment of ground targets. Built under license from Skywatch Aerospace Inc. and refit with Osprey's modular hardware, the Kelenken is a rugged and reliable airframe capable of weathering the Martian skies on long sorties.

Designed by Owlfeathers.

language_elements.name: prefab-desc-07
language_elements.value:
A medium patrol fighter designed for air superiority, interception, and light ground attack roles. The TA-07 is a license-built variant of the Skywatch Aerospace AS-07 Hawkmoth II, and sees use in the hands of both Osprey contractors and the company's own private security forces.

Designed by Owlfeathers.

language_elements.name: prefab-desc-08
language_elements.value:
The second generation of the Vulture heavyweight aerial ordnance platform designed to utilize the stability and low-speed performance of Flat Wings to deliver depleted uranium slugs anywhere, anytime, at any height. Prioritizes maintaining control and accuracy under heavy damages. Recommended for more experienced pilots.

Designed by N_Thinker.

language_elements.name: prefab-desc-09
language_elements.value:
A high-tech lightweight interceptor with a focus on harassing other aerial targets at long ranges. The Raycast does its best work at high speed, striking hard and fading before returning fire can catch it in the open. Recommended for more experienced pilots.

 Designed by BreakfastBaron.

Company credits are paid out for performance in battles.

You can spend your company credits on requisitions, parts, cosmetics, or prefabs.

Credits are capped at 100k, to encourage employee expenditure.

language_elements.name: currency-info-premium-desc

Premium chips are a special currency awarded through achievements, events, and external purchases.


in-game purchases for premium chips are disabled until we have a product we feel is worth monetizing.


Your support means the world to us, in any form.

language_elements.name: credit-cap-warning-desc
language_elements.value:
Osprey Modular Hardware regrets to inform you that you have hit the internal company credit cap alloted to Zulu-class employees. 

Consider spending your company credits on requisitions, parts, cosmetics, or prefabs.

language_elements.value:
LEFT CLICK to add a part to your purchase order.

RIGHT CLICK to sell a part from your inventory.

SHIFT + CLICK to add / sell 5x at a time.

CTRL + CLICK to add / sell 10x at a time.

Hit CONFIRM to process the order.

language_elements.name: autobuy-help-desc
language_elements.value:
When enabled, Osprey will automatically purchase parts as you place them in build mode. 

This deducts the associated credits from your account and adds the part to your inventory in real time.

NOTICE: Employee debts generated by auto-purchasing parts are non-refundable. Proceed with caution.

language_elements.name: techtree-wip-desc
language_elements.value:
The Osprey modular hardware research and development department is being researched...
and developed. come back later!

language_elements.name: tech-help-desc
language_elements.value:
Welcome to the Procelio Program Requisition Catalog. Here, you can spend your hard-earned company credits to unlock licenses to use new components on your bots. 

Each category has its own tree composed of nodes, and each node has prerequisites to fulfil. Click on a node to learn more about its contents and prerequisites. You can unlock nodes with credits once all of its prerequisites are met. Unlocking nodes unlocks their relevant equipment, items, or bonuses for your account, as well as provide access to nodes deeper in each tree.

When a piece of equipment is unlocked, it becomes available for use in the garage. Most basic parts are available with no limits, while more advanced parts such as modules and cosmetics may require additional copies to be purchased in the shop.

Resource Awards are one-time bonuses of items applied to your account. All sales are final.

language_elements.name: shared-prefabs-desc
language_elements.value: Upload bots to share with your friends list automatically. up to three bots can be shared at once. friends can view and download these bots.

language_elements.name: prefab-error-parts-unpurchased
language_elements.value: Prefab CONTAINS PARTS YOU DO NOT OWN

language_elements.name: prefab-error-parts-locked
language_elements.value: Prefab contains parts you have not unlocked

language_elements.name: prefab-error-failsafe
language_elements.value: PREFAB IS CORRUPTED OR INVALID- TELL DEVS

language_elements.name: confirm-prefab-import-desc
language_elements.value:
Are you sure you want to import prefab [prefabnamehere] into bay [baynumberhere]?

This action cannot be reversed.

language_elements.name: prefab-export-confirmation
language_elements.value: Robot saved to local backup folder.

language_elements.name: botfileimport-button
language_elements.value:
GOT OLD .bot FILES?
IMPORT THEM HERE!

language_elements.name: botfile-import-subheader
language_elements.value: AVAILABLE FOR A LIMITED TIME ONLY!

language_elements.name: botfile-import-desc
language_elements.value:
Thank you for playing Procelio during our Alpha phase! If you have any old .bot files exported from the proceliotool bot manager program, you can freely import them as local prefabs here. Local prefabs are our official in-game replacement for the old bot manager program.

To import your old files, copy the full file path leading either to the directory housing your bot files, or to a bot file directly, and paste it into the field below. If the bot files are located and valid, they will be turned into local prefabs for future import. 

Example 1: C:\Users\[YOU]\Documents\Your_Procelio_Bots
Example 2: C:\Users\[YOU]\Documents\Your_Procelio_Bots\myrobot.bot

Do note that you will need to unlock the parts on your robot before you can import it. This service will only be available for a limited time, and will be discontinued some time after our Steam launch, alongside the deprecation of the bot manager program.

language_elements.value: Have you suffered repeated losses when using an Osprey Modular Vehicle™ product? Try working on your driving and aiming skills, or request a new Modular Vehicle from our design team!

language_elements.name: infolog_267_help-body

language_elements.name: infolog_148_cosmetics-body
language_elements.value: The Craig cosmetic is not real and is a model. No isopods were harmed in the making of this project.

language_elements.name: infolog_171_atmospheric_warning-body
language_elements.value: To all planet-side employees, this your monthly reminder that Mars is cold, inhospitable, and has no breathable atmosphere. Don't get excited, and always remember to bring your suit.

language_elements.name: infolog_135_osprey_home-body
language_elements.value:
Did you know? 

It is illegal to wield a fire axe within the Osprey HOME Testing Environment™ without a license.

language_elements.name: infolog_137_osprey_home-body
language_elements.value:
Did you know? 

The Osprey HOME Testing Environment™ is not very energy efficient.

language_elements.name: infolog_0_adding_tooltips-body
language_elements.value:
Your Procelio software package comes with 'Loading Screen Tooltips' functionality.

While still in the prototype stage, Osprey Modular Hardware and Technology™ is committed to bringing you best-in-class tips and tricks with which to dominate your adversaries!

language_elements.name: infolog_38_sights-body
language_elements.value:
Right clicking with any Osprey weapon equipped will focus your AR rendering and zoom in for increased precision at a greater distance.

For a more mild zoom, press ${DriveZoom} to lower your Field of View on command, temporarily increasing mid-range precision.

language_elements.name: infolog_25_augmented_reality_controls-body
language_elements.value:
AR Technology made available to Osprey Modular Vehicle™ operators and designers allows you to visualize certain elements of bot metadata at the press of a button!

In Build and Test mode, open the Gizmos menu to see options to display important information like your vehicle's center of mass or collision boxes.

language_elements.name: infolog_11_electrolaser_mastery-body
language_elements.value: The Electrolaser Carbine (ELC) fires a continuous flurry of high energy bolts at the enemy, gaining inaccuracy over time. Hold off the trigger between shots to let the guns recalibrate, and tap the fire button to fire accurate shots over long distances.

language_elements.name: infolog_12_nova_cannon_mastery-body
language_elements.value:
The Nova Cannon fires intense volleys of superheated energy at the enemy. These volleys inherit some of your bots velocity and arc downwards with gravity, so plan accordingly. Nova shots explode on impact, dealing splash damage. 

Did you know? The reload times for Nova Launchers scale with the amount of guns on your robot.

language_elements.name: infolog_13_gauss_rifle_mastery-body
language_elements.value:
The Electromagnetic Gauss Rifle is a high precision tool of destruction. The rifle becomes unstable while moving, but the integrated laser sight will always shine true to where it's aiming. Learn to fire when you see it line up with your target.

The Gauss Rifle uses a shell-based magazine. Press ${DriveReload} to start reloading, and you can cancel any time after the first shell reloads.

language_elements.name: infolog_14_rivet_rifle_mastery-body
language_elements.value:
The Rivet Rifle was originally a tool for construction, but it fares equally as well in robot destruction. It pulls from a passively recharging magazine and can be fired semi automatically to maintain accuracy.

Hold fire to engage full auto mode, but keep in mind the fire pattern spreads quickly this way.

language_elements.name: infolog_17_missile_pod_mastery-body
language_elements.value: A versatile but simple secondary weapon, use these smart missiles to finish off low health enemies, or pressure stationary targets (such as Gauss snipers) into moving out of position.

language_elements.name: infolog_18_mine_layer_mastery-body
language_elements.value: Putting landmines in difficult to see places will surprise (damage) the enemy before engagement. They can also be used as improvised bombs on aircraft, but be mindful of their arm time.

language_elements.name: infolog_19_smoke_module_mastery-body
language_elements.value: Absolutely decimate the enemy by blinding them with smoke. Might also blind yourself.

language_elements.name: infolog_20_cas_cutter_mastery-body
language_elements.value:
These Contained Arc Saws will cut through anything, including other robots. Getting up close and personal, the cutter can output high amounts of damage in a flash, but puts the user's craft in danger. 

Consider escape routes before engaging.

language_elements.name: infolog_21_module_deck-body
language_elements.value: Multiple modules can be equipped at once, with their activation keys being shown next to the weapons module in the HUD. If a module is destroyed, an error sign will take its place.

language_elements.name: infolog_27_osprey_building_system-body
language_elements.value:
Did you know? 

It is possible to replace all primary weapons at once, by placing a new weapon of your choice on top of any of your existing weaponry

language_elements.name: infolog_28_osprey_test_simulacrum-body
language_elements.value:
Did you know? 

It is possible to switch between multiple testing environments within your Procelio software package settings.

language_elements.name: infolog_29_osprey_garage_screens-body
language_elements.value:
Did you know? 

It is possible to change the garage panel displays within your Procelio software package settings.

language_elements.name: infolog_31_osprey_logbook-body
language_elements.value:
Did you know? 

An Osprey Employee Logbook comes standard with your Procelio software package.

language_elements.name: infolog_32_musical_motivation-body
language_elements.value:
Did you know? 

You are able to add custom music to your radio.

language_elements.name: infolog_33_flat_wings-body
language_elements.value: Flat wings provide great redundancy with their cheap cost and provide good control at low speeds, but are prone to overspeed. It will take longer to turn at speed without outside forces.

language_elements.name: infolog_33_swept_wings-body
language_elements.value: Swept wings provide a great middle ground between ease of use, control, and lift, at the cost of price. If you're not sure what kind of plane to build, consider starting here.

language_elements.name: infolog_35_delta_wings-body
language_elements.value: Looking for some high speed action? Delta wings provide high control and lift performance at speed, but are more prone to lose control during sharp turns and stalls.

language_elements.name: infolog_36_rudders-body
language_elements.value:
Rudders provide more control and stability surfaces for your aircraft to work with, but do not provide much lift.

All rudders provide the same control benefits.

language_elements.name: infolog_37_flight-body
language_elements.value: Making a flying bot requires wings, thrust, and balance. Your bot will try to split lift and other forces across multiple wings to maintain stable flight, but flying bots are ultimately more fragile than grounded ones.

language_elements.name: infolog_38_tank_tracks-body
language_elements.value:
Tank tracks provide excellent armor for their power requirements, at the cost of an increased mass. While more sluggish in the long run compared to other ground movement types, tanks make up for this in their superior in-combat maneuvering, on-the-spot turning, and hill climbning ability. 
Hold ${RobotDriveDown} to enter high gear.

language_elements.name: infolog_39_tank_gears-body
language_elements.value: When using Tank Tracks, hold ${RobotDriveDown} to enter high gear. While in high gear, your direct drive power and speed is greatly increased, at the cost of turning ability. Use it to cover long distances outside of combat, or for a faster escape. Be wary of the short cooldown when switching gears.

language_elements.name: infolog_41_advanced_build_mode-body
language_elements.value: While in the garage, hold down ${GarageEnableAdvanced} to enter advanced build mode. When in advanced mode, you can use various keys to adjust block position and rotation in 3D space, without needing to bridge off of existing parts.

language_elements.name: infolog_42_inertial_rectifier-body
language_elements.value:
While in control of your robot, press ${DriveFlipBot} to gain a vertical boost and align your bot upright. 

Alternatively, hold down ${DriveFlipBot} to control your bots alignment rotation and release to drop your robot in the desired direction.

language_elements.name: infolog_43_iff_tagging-body
language_elements.value:
Your AR HUD comes equiped with a state-of-the-art Identification Friend or Foe (IFF) system. Osprey's SatOPS teams automatically manage assigning friendly units to your on-screen IFF. 

Press ${BattleEntitySpot} while looking at an unknown unit to mark them as a hostile for both yourself and your allies. Spotted enemies are trackable through walls, but will eventually lose their mark.
 NOTE: Mis-identifying a unit or attempting to identify rocks as enemies will require time for the system to recalibrate, and will be marked on your annual performance review.

language_elements.name: infolog_892_painting-body
language_elements.value:
Osprey Modular Hardware and Technology™ provides tools to paint your Osprey Modular Vehicle™ in many different colors. 

Designers may create harmonic gradient palettes.

language_elements.name: infolog_5694_wheels-body
language_elements.value:
Osprey Modular Hardware and Technology™'s wheels are able to be placed on your Osprey Modular Vehicle™. 

A single wheel is able to put out around one thousand brake horsepower.

language_elements.name: infolog_7922_terrain-body
language_elements.value:
Your Osprey Modular Vehicle™, if built properly, may traverse many different types of terrain. 

Consider your movement parts well, especially when crossing rocky terrain.

language_elements.name: infolog_1_build_zone-body
language_elements.value: The Build Environment is an Osprey-deployed space outpost for mixed-reality construction and testing of Osprey Modular Vehicle™ products. Once a robot's design passes client-requested quality assurance testing, the robot is manufactured and shipped planetside.

language_elements.name: infolog_3_neutrality-body
language_elements.value: Osprey Modular Hardware and Technology™ does not condone the actions of either Hypox™ or the Aegis Frontiersmen in the ongoing conflict on Mars.

language_elements.name: infolog_6_corporate_partnerships-body
language_elements.value:
Osprey Modular Hardware and Technology™ is proud to announce its partnership with SpaceTek Dynamics™ to bring you canisters ideal for use with SpaceTek's LighterThanAir™ products.

WARNING: ONCE LTA CANISTER IS PRESSURIZED, DO NOT SHAKE, HEAT, OR PUNCTURE.

language_elements.name: infolog_9_work_conditions-body
language_elements.value: In light of recently-publicized claims, Osprey Modular Hardware and Technology™ would like to remind you that all Innovation Laborers™ are paid a fair wage and are typically permitted to return to their families on weekends.

language_elements.name: infolog_5_alternative_products-body
language_elements.value: In addition to Modular Vehicles™, Osprey provides a number of products designed to make your life easier. Try our Wuud™ collection, wood-like products made from a wide selection of organic and inorganic materials!

language_elements.name: infolog_7_captain_procelio-body
language_elements.value: Despite rumors to the contrary, Osprey Modular Hardware and Technology™ has no association with the individual known as 'Captain Procelio' and has no information as to his whereabouts.

language_elements.name: infolog_8_proceli-os-body
language_elements.value: In collaboration with One Mars Radio™, Osprey Modular Hardware and Technology™'s Classified Research and Development Division is proud to announce the public release of "Proceli-Os™": Part of a Healthy and Heavy-Metal-Free Breakfast!

language_elements.name: infolog_10_store-of-value-body
language_elements.value: As to better facilitate interplanetary commerce, Osprey Modular Hardware and Technology™ has begun work on the Future Initiative for Space-based Holdings, a commodity-backed currency project for engaging in stable trade of Osprey™ (and partners)'s goods without reliance on government fiat.

language_elements.name: infolog_25_shipping_services-body
language_elements.value:
Osprey Modular Hardware and Technology™ will ship anything, anywhere, fast and safe.

Osprey would like to remind all employees that shipping with Voyant is forbidden outside of raw construction material shipments.

language_elements.name: ma_tollum-body
language_elements.value:
'Treaty Organization for the Legislation and Land Use of Mars.'

Formed by Earth governments at the turn of the 2100's, TOLLUM holds responsibility for alloting and tracking legal distribution and ownership of Martian soil. The Hypox Mining Company™ lays claim to land granted to it for exclusive use by TOLLUM.

language_elements.name: ma_hypox_mining_company-body
language_elements.value: Hypox Mining Company™ set foot on Mars, after winning mineral rights from TOLLUM, to extract valuable minerals from the red planet. While investors expected straightforward operations with good returns, their hopes were stymied by the discovery of unlicensed colonies already established on lands now belonging to Hypox™.

language_elements.name: ma_osprey_modular_hardware-body
language_elements.value: The foremost entity in private space travel and industry -- rivaling even the United Defense Force's in heavy industry -- Osprey™ and its myriad subdivisions have the ability to manufacture and transport virtually anything under the sun.

language_elements.name: ma_osprey_robotics-body
language_elements.value: When peaceful efforts to resettle illegal colonies on Hypox™'s land were met with a string of incidents of property damage and vandalism, the mining company contracted with Osprey™'s robotics subdivision to dispatch autonomous and remote-controlled drones to protect its property against hostile tresspassers.

language_elements.name: ma_aegis-body
language_elements.value:
AEGIS Colonization Enterprises™ was established in the early days of Martian colonization (preceding most regulation), offering paid trips and supplies for prospective Martian colonists in exchange for indentured servitude in colony-based production chains.

AEGIS, in cooperation with both governments and private enterprise, has had a hand in not only the majority of colonial settlement, but also in much Martian infrastructure on the whole.

language_elements.name: ma_aegis_frontiersmen-body
language_elements.value:
A militia of hardened survivors who came to Mars seeking new lives and new opportunities under the AEGIS Martian Colonization Initiative™ who, after completing their contractural period of servitude, set out to claim their own land and form their own settlements. As a result, the Frontiersmen have found themselves in direct conflict with Hypox™.
While lacking the formal backing of any Martian organizations, the group has managed to acquire access to the same modular robotics technology from Osprey that Hypox has.

language_elements.name: ma_spacetek_dynamics-body
language_elements.value: SpaceTek Dynamics™, one of the leading spacing corporations, specializes in high-tech research and development, exceeding by far Osprey's R&D teams in applied physics research. While the privately-held company is generally secretive about its dealings, it has in the past partnered with AEGIS Colonization Enterprises™ to build and run the Icarus North Massive Solar Array, and the Deimos Skyhook Project.

language_elements.name: tollum_(for_dummies)-body
language_elements.value:
'Treaty Organization for the Legislation and Land Use of Mars.'

TOLLUM was created by Earth governments in the early 2100s to manage land ownership on Mars. It keeps track of who owns what land, with the Hypox Mining Company™ receiving exclusive rights to certain areas from TOLLUM.

language_elements.name: hypox_mining_company_(for_dummies)-body
language_elements.value: The Hypox Mining Company™ came to Mars after getting the right to mine minerals from TOLLUM. They ran into trouble when they found illegal colonies on the land now controlled by Hypox™.

language_elements.name: osprey_modular_hardware_(for_dummies)-body
language_elements.value: Osprey™ is a major player in private space travel and industry, even competing with the United Defense Force in the heavy industry sector. Its many divisions can manufacture and transport almost anything needed for space operations.

language_elements.name: osprey_robotics_(for_dummies)-body
language_elements.value: When Hypox™ faced vandalism and damage to equipment while trying to move illegal colonies off their land, they hired Osprey™'s robotics division to send out drones to protect their property from trespassers.

language_elements.name: aegis_(for_dummies)-body
language_elements.value: AEGIS Colonization Enterprises™ was created early in Mars' settlement, before most laws were in place. They provided settlers with transportation and supplies in exchange for work. AEGIS played a big role in helping to settle Mars and build its infrastructure.

language_elements.name: aegis_frontiersmen_(for_dummies)-body
language_elements.value: The Frontiersmen are a group of former workers who came to Mars under AEGIS. After finishing their service, they chose to settle Mars on their own and came into conflict with Hypox™. They also gained access to the same advanced robotics from Osprey™ that Hypox™ uses.

language_elements.name: spacetek_dynamics_(for_dummies)-body
language_elements.value: SpaceTek Dynamics™ is a leading space research company, more advanced than Osprey™ in applied physics. Though secretive, they have worked with AEGIS on major projects like the Icarus North Solar Array and Deimos Skyhook Project.

language_elements.name: mars_travel_guide_trains-body
language_elements.value: The Red Sand Rail Company, in tight partnership with AEGIS, is responsible for maintaining the high velocity maglev trains that carry goods, cargo, and materials from point to point across Mars. With a maximum surface operating velocity exceeding 3000kph (due to the rarified Martian atmosphere), these reinforced trains are renowned for keeping a tight schedule regardless of weather or debris.

language_elements.name: mars_travel_guide_alpha_prospect-body
language_elements.value: Alpha Prospect is one of the first mining operations established by Hypox in pursuit of rich niobium ores. While largely controlled by Hypox, the mine has become a symbolic landmark and a focal point of Frontiersmen' attacks against Hypox's operations.

language_elements.name: mars_radio_guide_deimos_nexus-body
language_elements.value: Deimos Nexus is the first and largest Spaceport on Mars. Constructed by AEGIS and SpaceTek under the management of TOLLUM, it serves as a massive shipping and immigration center for the red planet. While designated to be neutral ground by Earth nations, even it is not safe from the proxy war on Mars.

language_elements.name: mars_travel_guide_icarus_north-body
language_elements.value: The Icarus North Solar Array is a monumental solar farm built into the Planum Boreale of Mars in a partnership between AEGIS™ and SpaceTek Dynamics™. During the summer season, the massive facility uses rail-mounted solar panels to chase the never-setting polar sun to supposedly generate energy. During the winter months, a complement of SpaceTek's scientists remain at the facility to ensure it remains in good working order.Among other things...

language_elements.name: mars_travel_guide_cebrenia-body
language_elements.value: Despite the widely-popular drone recordings of the Cebrenia quadrangle's Hecates Thelus volcano, access to the quadrant remains strictly prohibited by Martian authorities in the wake of the mysterious implosion in 21XX
